steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The preceding sentence establishes a general fact: of 206 ticker-tape parades, "a number were for achievements in aviation." The blank introduces the next sentence, which gives a specific example: Corrigan's parade was one of 16 honoring pilots. The transition must signal that this specific detail confirms or reinforces the general claim just made.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — In addition,: signals that separate or additional information follows — but Corrigan's parade is not a new point; it is evidence for the claim already made, so this transition is too weak. ✗
  • Choice B — Therefore,: signals a logical consequence or conclusion — but the specific count of 16 aviation parades does not follow as a consequence of the general statement; it supports it. ✗
  • Choice C — Indeed,: signals confirmation or reinforcement of a preceding claim — it tells the reader that what follows validates and strengthens what was just stated generally. ✓
  • Choice D — Nevertheless,: signals contrast or a concession — but Corrigan's being one of 16 aviation honorees does not contradict the claim that some parades honored aviation; it agrees with it. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
